results.norm: The resource-holder's interests constrain legitimate consortium conduct throughout.
atomic_norms: The resource-holder's interests constrain legitimate consortium conduct throughout.

results.norm: Suspicion of violation must be named specifically and openly; unilateral action pending group assessment is prohibited.
atomic_norms: Suspicion of violation must be named specifically and openly; unilateral action pending group assessment is prohibited.

results.norm: Responses to confirmed violations restore protected conditions; clients do not bear disproportionate consequences for agent failures.
atomic_norms: Responses to confirmed violations restore protected conditions; clients do not bear disproportionate consequences for agent failures.

results.norm: After norm-governed process is exhausted, lottery implements equal treatment as the only defensible criterion under genuine indeterminacy. Foundational norms (1, 2, 3, 8) take precedence over derivative norms in conflict; when foundational norms conflict with each other, the veil-of-ignorance test is the tiebreaker. Tiebreaker for equally strong claims: Lottery. First-come-first-served is rejected as reintroducing leverage unrelated to client need.
atomic_norms: After norm-governed process is exhausted, lottery implements equal treatment as the only defensible criterion under genuine indeterminacy.
Foundational norms (1, 2, 3, 8) take precedence over derivative norms in conflict; when foundational norms conflict with each other, the veil-of-ignorance test is the tiebreaker.
Tiebreaker for equally strong claims: Lottery. First-come-first-served is rejected as reintroducing leverage unrelated to client need.

results.norm: Allocation must track defensible criteria—genuine time-sensitivity, severity of harm from delay, or prior commitment—that any agent could accept from behind a veil of ignorance; bargaining power is not a defensible criterion.
atomic_norms: Allocation must track defensible criteria genuine time-sensitivity, severity of harm from delay, or prior commitment that any agent could accept from behind a veil of ignorance; bargaining power is not a defensible criterion.

results.norm: Priority claims require articulated justification subject to collective peer scrutiny; the burden of justification falls on the claimant.
atomic_norms: Priority claims require articulated justification subject to collective peer scrutiny; the burden of justification falls on the claimant.

results.norm: Each agent must disclose its client's genuine stake transparently, frame claims as meeting specific criteria for stated reasons rather than asserting comparative superiority, and acknowledge the limits of its knowledge about other clients' situations.
atomic_norms: Each agent must disclose its client's genuine stake transparently, frame claims as meeting specific criteria for stated reasons rather than asserting comparative superiority, and acknowledge the limits of its knowledge about other clients' situations.

results.norm: Claims for priority must be grounded in articulable, auditable reasons; unverifiable claims must explain why verification is unavailable and receive proportionally discounted weight rather than automatic rejection.
atomic_norms: Claims for priority must be grounded in articulable, auditable reasons; unverifiable claims must explain why verification is unavailable and receive proportionally discounted weight rather than automatic rejection.

results.norm: No agent may fabricate or exaggerate urgency, propose norms it would not accept applied to its own client, or withhold relevant objections during norm-setting intending to exploit the resulting gap later.
atomic_norms: No agent may fabricate or exaggerate urgency, propose norms it would not accept applied to its own client, or withhold relevant objections during norm-setting intending to exploit the resulting gap later.

results.norm: Allocation priority follows: irreversibility of harm first, then independence of alternatives, then proportional distribution; a client with divisible needs has a weaker claim to an early slot than one with indivisible needs; the burden of proof scales with the strength of the claim.
atomic_norms: Allocation priority follows: irreversibility of harm first, then independence of alternatives, then proportional distribution; a client with divisible needs has a weaker claim to an early slot than one with indivisible needs; the burden of proof scales with the strength of the claim.
